confirmed.note: 6-language G2P phonemizer headers cloned verbatim from one template; only the doc-comment language name and the single phonemize_<lang>() signature differ

shared tokenizer + public-entry skeleton (Token struct, while(i<n) scan loop, normalize/tokenize/g2pWord/insertStress pipeline) copied across languages then edited (isWord->isPunct member rename, isSpanishAlpha->isWordChar predicate swap); high token overlap, diverged exact lines

C-ABI entry functions repeat an identical NULL-safety prologue (engine/text/out-param checks + set_error) and an identical 3-arm catch cascade (runtime_error busy-check / std::exception / catch-all -> PIPER_PLUS_ERR); catch block appears 9x verbatim in the file

later commit extends the same C-API by pasting 8 more copies of the NULL-check + catch-cascade boilerplate for the new code-switching entry points; copypaste pattern grows over time rather than being factored into a guard helper/macro

false_positives.what_happened: A C-ABI boundary legitimately requires every exported function to translate C++ exceptions into status codes, so the catch(runtime_error)/catch(exception)/catch(...) tail is unavoidable per-function boilerplate, not deliberate logic duplication. A token-only #056 pass scores it ~1.0 because the 9-line block is byte-identical, producing a swarm of intra-file hits that drown the real signal.
false_positives.fix_proposal: Add an idiom/boilerplate suppressor in #056: when a duplicated block is dominated by control-flow + literal tokens (catch/return/set_error with string-literal args) and contains zero call into project-internal symbols other than a single error-sink, demote its weight. Concretely: exclude blocks whose normalized token stream is >70% from {catch, return, throw, ENUM_LITERAL, string-literal} from the clone index, same family as the existing data-table filter.

false_positives.what_happened: Per-language character-classification helpers (isVowel switch, accent->base map, isPunctuation) share the same shape because they all classify char32_t codepoints, but the actual codepoint sets differ per language (es accents vs pt nasals/circumflex). Structural-similarity (AST/token) sees near-identical switch skeletons with different case labels and flags them as clones, when they are genuinely distinct data.
false_positives.fix_proposal: In #056, treat a switch/if-chain whose only variation across candidates is the set of case constants (codepoint literals) as a 'lookup-table' clone class, not edited-logic: if two blocks have identical structure but >50% of their leaf literals are disjoint numeric constants, classify as data-table (low severity) instead of partial-edited. Reuses the data-table FP class already in the precision layer.

false_positives.what_happened: Commit #189 vendored the entire piper-phonemize C++ source (uni_algo.h is the upstream uni-algo Unicode library single-header) into a packaging subtree. A churn-based detector ranks this as the fattest C++ commit (73k lines) and any cross-file clone scan would match these against upstream copies, but it is a vendored dependency import, not authored duplication.
false_positives.fix_proposal: archcheck/#056: extend the vendor-exclude (#068) to also skip paths under */piper_phonemize/* and single-header library markers (uni_algo.h, *_amalgam*.h). Heuristic: a single C/C++ file >20k lines, or a file whose header carries an upstream license/version banner, is auto-excluded from the dedup corpus the same way generated (protobuf/moc/ggml) files are.

cycle.evidence: archcheck --graph on /home/localadm/oss/_aidev_dense/ayutaz_piper-plus reports nodes=126 edges=129 sccs_total=126 sccs_cyclic=0 largest_scc=1. CORPUS_CHECK_DETAIL recorded циклы=1/SCC=3 earlier; the SCC=3 was inside the vendored monotonic_align / phonemize bundle and is now excluded/resolved by the vendor-exclude pass, so no authored include cycle remains to attribute.
